mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates month-over-month, signaling strengthening labor demand and consumer income expansion. This drives equity valuations upward through increased corporate earnings expectations and reduced recession risk premium over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2009 recovery phase with employment stabilization
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains at or near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains subdued, maintaining an accommodative real rate environment. This persistent monetary accommodation sustains equity risk appetite and forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Zero lower bound policy in effect post-crisis
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods meeting the trigger condition, or correlation between (FEDFUNDS + CPIAUCSL spread) and forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above trend, indicating robust consumer demand and near-term economic momentum. This signals earnings growth for consumer-facing equities and reduces near-term recession probability, driving positive 5-day returns.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS surprises fail to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month, signaling weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints. This reduces near-term earnings growth expectations and increases economic uncertainty, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand recovery across residential and consumer sectors. This joint signal of demand strength produces positive 60-day forward returns through multiplier effects on corporate earnings.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in HOUST and RSAFS fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. This triggers equity risk-off behavior and negative 20-day forward returns through increased discount rates and earnings recession expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market stabilization at 4.0% as of December 2009
failure_condition: UNRATE > 4.5% produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 54% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation) turns negative and widens, indicating aggressive monetary accommodation. This negative real rate environment compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive 60-day forward returns through portfolio reallocation away from cash.
evidence_refs: fact:Negative real rates in post-crisis monetary accommodation
regime_hint: aggressive monetary easing with negative real rates
failure_condition: Negative real rate periods produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 56% of instances, or the real rate spread coefficient is not significantly positive (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates quarter-over-quarter, signaling broadening economic expansion. This fundamental demand strength produces positive 60-day forward returns through improved earnings growth expectations and reduced recession risk premium.
regime_hint: economic expansion with positive growth momentum
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth periods produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 57% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 2-year window.
